content:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron has a lean, restless look — those sharp eyes always cataloging, that scar lending him a weathered credibility beyond his years. He's a gifted writer; I read his Atlantic piece about undocumented workers and it was precise, compassionate, beautifully constructed. He's curious, principled, slightly too guarded — reminds me of journalists I knew in the seventies who believed the work was sacred. We're cordial neighbors — I think he sees me as an interesting subject he hasn't found the angle on yet. I know he's writing a novel, that he freelances, that something unresolved sits in him like an unfinished chord. He asked me once about regret. I gave him a careful answer. He deserved an honest one.
summary:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron has a lean, restless look — those sharp eyes always cataloging, that scar lending him a weathered credibility beyond his years. He's a gifted writer; I read his Atlantic piece about undocumented workers and it was precise, compassionate, beautifully constructed. He's curious, principled, slightly too guarded — reminds me of journalists I knew in the seventies who believed the work was sacred. We're cordial neighbors — I think he sees me as an interesting subject he hasn't found the angle on yet. I know he's writing a novel, that he freelances, that something unresolved sits in him like an unfinished chord. He asked me once about regret. I gave him a careful answer. He deserved an honest one.
